title: Act of 13 July 1999 on liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle and on amendment of certain related acts (Act on motor third party liability insurance)

parts.Změna zákona č. 12/1997 Sb., o bezpečnos: Amendment to Act No. 12/1997 Sb., on the safety and fluidity of road traffic
parts.Změna zákona č. 185/1991 Sb., o pojišťov: Amendment to Act No. 185/1991 Sb., on insurance, as amended

p1.text:
(1) This Act incorporates the relevant regulations of the European Communities^1) and regulates

- a) liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle (hereinafter referred to as „liability insurance“),

- b) the establishment of the Czech Insurers’ Bureau (hereinafter referred to as the „Bureau“), its legal status, organisation, and scope of activity.

(2) Unless this Act stipulates otherwise,

- a) in the case of a vehicle registered in the road vehicle register pursuant to the Act regulating the conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads, the obligation of liability insurance under this Act must be fulfilled for the entire period during which the vehicle is registered in the road vehicle register, with the exception of the period when it is registered in the road vehicle register as decommissioned, exported to another state, or defunct, and the period when the vehicle is stolen,

- b) only a person whose obligation to compensate for damage caused by the operation of such vehicle is insured under this Act may operate a vehicle on a motorway, road, local road, and special-purpose road, with the exception of a special-purpose road that is not publicly accessible (hereinafter referred to as „road“); the obligation of liability insurance must also be fulfilled in the event of leaving a vehicle on a road.

(3) Unless this Act stipulates otherwise, liability insurance is governed by the Civil Code.

p2.heading: Definition of terms
p2.text:
For the purposes of this Act,

- a) „vehicle“ means a road vehicle, special vehicle, trolleybus^5); a wheelchair, animal-drawn vehicle, and non-motorised vehicle pulled or pushed by a pedestrian, bicycle, and scooter are not considered vehicles, unless approved as a type of motorcycle vehicle,

- b) „domestic vehicle“ means a vehicle subject to road vehicle registration maintained pursuant to the Act on the conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads^9) (hereinafter referred to as „vehicle registration“), or a vehicle not subject to vehicle registration and owned by a natural person with residence or place of business in the territory of the Czech Republic or a legal entity with its registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic, or a vehicle that has been sent to the Czech Republic from another Member State [letter l)], if the Czech Republic is the state of destination, for a period of 30 days starting from the day the buyer took possession of the vehicle, even if the vehicle was not registered in the Czech Republic,

- c) „foreign vehicle“ means a vehicle equipped with a foreign registration plate, or a vehicle not equipped with a foreign registration plate and owned by a natural person with residence or place of business outside the territory of the Czech Republic, or a legal entity with its registered office outside the territory of the Czech Republic,

- d) „insurer“ means an insurance company that is authorised to operate liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic pursuant to a special legal regulation^10),

- e) „policyholder“ means the person who concluded a liability insurance contract with the insurer,

- f) „insured person“ means the person whose obligation to compensate for damage is covered by the liability insurance,

- g) „injured party“ means the person to whom damage was caused by the operation of a vehicle and who has the right to compensation for damage,

- h) „insured event“ means the causing of damage by the operation of a vehicle,

- i) „bureau of insurers“ means a professional organisation associating insurance companies that are authorised to operate liability insurance in a certain state,

- j) „registration authority“ means the administrative authority competent for vehicle registration pursuant to the Act on the conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads,

- k) „Green Card“ means an international certificate proving that a liability insurance contract for damage caused by the operation of the vehicle specified in this certificate has been concluded,

- l) „Member State“ means a Member State of the European Union or another state forming the European Economic Area,

- m) „territory where the vehicle is normally based“ means

- 1. the territory of the state whose registration plate or other registration mark the vehicle bears, regardless of whether the said mark is permanent or temporary, or

- 2. the territory of the state in which the mark assigned to the vehicle by the insurer or another distinguishing mark was issued, if the vehicle is not subject to vehicle registration, but the vehicle bears a mark assigned by the insurer or another distinguishing mark that is analogous to a registration plate, or

- 3. the territory of the state in which the owner or co-owner (hereinafter referred to as „owner“) of the vehicle resides, if neither a registration plate nor a mark assigned by the insurer or another distinguishing mark that is analogous to a registration plate is required for such a vehicle, or

- 4. the territory of the state where the insured event occurred, if it is a vehicle without a registration plate, even though it should have been equipped with such a plate, or a vehicle with a registration plate or other registration mark that does not correspond to or has ceased to correspond to the vehicle, in cases of performance provided pursuant to § 24 para. 2 letter b).

Part Two

Liability Insurance

p3.heading: Insurance contract
p3.text:
(1) Liability insurance arises on the basis of an insurance contract concluded between the policyholder and the insurer. The insurer is always obliged to conclude an insurance contract if the proposal for its conclusion does not contravene this Act, the Act regulating insurance contracts, or the insurer's insurance terms and conditions. In the insurance contract, the contracting parties may deviate from this Act only if such deviation does not lead to a reduction or limitation of the injured party's claims arising from the right to compensation for damage from liability insurance caused by the operation of a vehicle. Liability insurance cannot be suspended^11) unless otherwise agreed in the insurance contract. If the suspension of liability insurance has been agreed in the insurance contract, the policyholder is obliged, before the commencement of the insurance suspension, to prove to the insurer that the vehicle has been taken out of operation in accordance with the Act on the conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads, if it is a vehicle subject to vehicle registration. During the period of suspension of liability insurance, the obligation of liability insurance is not fulfilled.

(2) The owner of a domestic vehicle or the driver of a foreign vehicle is obliged to conclude an insurance contract for liability insurance, unless this Act stipulates otherwise. This does not affect the right to conclude such a contract as insurance for a third-party insurance risk. One liability insurance contract may insure liability arising from the operation of multiple vehicles if they have the same owner, or the same operator, or if the policyholder has an insurable interest in concluding the contract based on the provision of a loan or other similar financial service, the purpose of which is the acquisition of ownership of the vehicle, or if the owner or operator of the vehicle and the policyholder are part of the same concern.

(3) Immediately after the conclusion of an insurance contract concerning a domestic vehicle, the insurer shall issue a green card to the policyholder.

(4) The insurance contract shall always contain:

- a) the identification of the insurer and the policyholder and information about the vehicle,

- b) the duration of the insurance,

- c) the limit of insurance benefits,

- d) the amount of the insurance premium, its due date, and method of payment, and

- e) the form and place for reporting an insured event; if liability insurance is operated in the form of temporary provision of services, also the address of residence or place of business, or the registered office of the claims representative according to § 9b.

(5) If, at the time of concluding the insurance contract or its amendment, some vehicle data are not known, but this does not prevent its identification, the policyholder shall communicate them to the insurer within the period specified by the insurer, but no later than 15 days from the date of concluding the insurance contract or its amendment. The policyholder shall notify the insurer of any change in data concerning the policyholder and the vehicle within 15 days from the date on which such change occurred.

p3a.heading: Limits of Insurance Benefits
p3a.text:
(1) For the purposes of this Act, the “limit of insurance benefits” means the maximum amount of the insurer's payment for a single insured event. The limits of insurance benefits must be stated separately in the insurance contract, in a manner corresponding to the breakdown of insurance benefit limits specified in paragraph 2.

(2) The limit of insurance benefits must correspond to:

- a) in the case of damage under § 6 para. 2 letter a), at least CZK 35,000,000 for each injured or deceased person, including compensation for costs incurred for care covered by public health insurance and the recourse claim under § 6 para. 4,

- b) in the case of damage under § 6 para. 2 letters b) and c), at least CZK 35,000,000 regardless of the number of injured parties; if the sum of claims asserted by multiple injured parties exceeds the limit of insurance benefits stated in the insurance contract, the insurance benefit for each of them shall be reduced in proportion to this limit to the sum of the claims of all injured parties.

(3) Within the limit under paragraph 2 letter b), costs of intervention by the Fire and Rescue Service of the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as the „fire and rescue service“) and municipal volunteer fire brigade units shall be covered under § 6 para. 4, if the intervention is related to damage under § 6 para. 2.

p3b.heading: Determination of the Insurance Premium Amount
p3b.text:
(1) The insurer is obliged to determine the amount of the insurance premium in accordance with the conditions stipulated by the Act regulating insurance business, so as to ensure the continuous solvency of obligations arising from the operation of liability insurance and the payment of contributions to the Office according to § 18 para. 5.

(2) When agreeing on the amount of the insurance premium in the insurance contract, the insurer shall take into account the policyholder's overall previous claims history of liability insurance, by granting a discount on the premium in the event of a claims-free insurance period or an additional charge to the premium in the event of payment of insurance benefits from liability insurance. The insurance contract may stipulate that the claims history of the person operating the vehicle shall be taken into account, if this person is different from the policyholder. When taking into account the previous claims history, the insurer shall not consider the period of suspension of liability insurance. In such a case, when taking into account the previous claims history, the period of suspension of liability insurance shall not be included in the insurance period of this insurance. The insurer shall submit the method of determining the amount of the insurance premium to the Czech National Bank for inspection upon its written request, including the statistical data on which the method of determining the amount of the insurance premium is based.

p4.heading: Contribution
p4.text:
(1) The owner of a domestic vehicle and its operator (hereinafter referred to as the „person obliged to pay the contribution“) are jointly and severally obliged to pay the Bureau a contribution for each day of breach of the obligation under § 1 para. 2; this does not apply if the vehicle has been stolen.

(2) When determining the person obliged to pay the contribution, it is presumed that

- a) the person registered as the owner of the vehicle in the road vehicle register is the owner of the vehicle, and

- b) the person registered as the operator of the vehicle in the road vehicle register is the operator of the vehicle.

(3) The amount of the contribution shall be calculated as the product of the number of days during which the obligation under § 1 para. 2 was breached, and the amount of the daily rate according to the type of vehicle.

(4) The person obliged to pay the contribution is obliged to pay the Bureau the costs associated with the out-of-court enforcement of the right to the contribution.

(5) The amount of the daily rate of the contribution, the types of vehicles for the purpose of the daily rate of the contribution, and the amount of the Bureau's costs associated with the out-of-court enforcement of the right to the contribution under paragraph 4 shall be stipulated by the Ministry of Finance by decree.

(6) The obligation to pay the contribution and the Bureau's costs under paragraph 4 shall cease if the Bureau does not send a request for payment of the contribution under paragraph 7 to the person obliged to pay the contribution within 1 year from the date of the creation of the obligation to pay the contribution, if it concerns a person registered as the owner or operator of the vehicle in the road vehicle register. In other cases, the 1-year period shall not begin to run before the Bureau demonstrably learns who the owner or operator of the vehicle is.

(7) The request for payment of the contribution must contain a description of the reasons for the creation of the right to the contribution, a quantification of the amount of the contribution and the Bureau's costs, a period for fulfilling the obligation to pay the contribution of at least 30 days from the date of delivery of the request, instruction to the addressee about their rights and obligations including the possibility to provide the Bureau with circumstances precluding the creation of the right to the contribution, and instruction about the Bureau's possibility to assert the right to payment of the contribution and the Bureau's costs in court.

p5.heading: Exemptions from liability insurance
p5.text:
(1) The obligation to conclude an insurance contract under this Act does not apply to

- a) a driver of a foreign vehicle who is a holder of a valid green card issued by an insurance company in a foreign state, or of a vehicle whose liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic is guaranteed by the bureau of insurers of a foreign state,

- b) a component of the integrated rescue system for all vehicles not used for business and included in the continuously updated annex to the alarm plan of the regional integrated rescue system,^12)

- c) the Security Information Service for vehicles operated by it, the General Inspection of Security Forces for vehicles operated by it, the Ministry of Defence for vehicles operated by Military Intelligence, and the Ministry of the Interior for vehicles operated by the Office for Foreign Relations and Information and for vehicles of units of the Police of the Czech Republic entrusted with investigation under a special legal regulation^12a), a municipality for vehicles operated by units of voluntary fire brigades of the municipality, and the Ministry of Finance (hereinafter referred to as „the Ministry“) for vehicles operated by authorized customs authorities which have the status of a police authority under a special legal regulation^12a).

(2) For a vehicle specified in paragraph 1 letter b) and c) (hereinafter referred to as „vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance“), the Ministry shall issue a green card. If the reason for which the green card was issued to a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance ceases to exist, the person operating such a vehicle is obliged to return the green card to the Ministry.

Scope of liability insurance

p6.text:
(1) Liability insurance covers every person who is obliged to compensate for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle specified in the insurance contract.

(2) Unless this Act stipulates otherwise, the insured person has the right to have the insurer pay the injured party, on their behalf, to the extent and amount specified by the Civil Code, for

- a) damage caused by bodily injury or death,

- b) purposefully incurred costs associated with the health care of an injured animal and damage caused by damage, destruction, or loss of property, as well as damage caused by theft of property, if a natural person has lost the ability to care for it,

- c) lost profit,

- d) purposefully incurred costs associated with legal representation in asserting claims under letters a) to c); however, in connection with damage under letter b) or c), only in the event of the fruitless expiry of the period under § 9 para. 3 or an unauthorized refusal or unauthorized reduction of insurance benefits by the insurer,

provided that the injured party has asserted and proven their claim and that the insured event, from which this damage arose and which the insured person is obliged to compensate, occurred during the term of the liability insurance, with the exception of the period of its suspension.

(3) Unless otherwise agreed, the insurer shall provide performance in the scope and amount according to paragraph 2 in money up to the limit of the insurance benefit specified in the insurance contract.

(4) The insured person further has the right to have the insurer pay, on their behalf, the claim asserted and proven by the relevant health insurance company for reimbursement of costs incurred for health care covered by public health insurance according to the Act regulating public health insurance^15a), if the health insurance company incurred these costs for health care provided to the injured party, provided that the insured event, from which this damage arose and which the insured person is obliged to compensate, occurred during the term of the liability insurance, with the exception of the period of its suspension. This applies mutatis mutandis also in the case of a recourse claim prescribed for payment to the insured person according to the Act regulating sickness insurance^15b) and in the case of reimbursement of costs of the fire and rescue service or units of voluntary fire brigades of the municipality according to § 3a para. 3.

(5) An insurance contract concerning liability insurance must be concluded so that this insurance, based on a single premium, is valid in the territory of all member states and other states listed in the list of states determined by the Ministry by decree. The insurer is obliged to indicate this validity of the insurance on the green card.

(6) Liability insurance is also valid in the territory of states other than those listed in paragraph 5, if the insurer has indicated this validity of the insurance on the green card.

(7) In the event of damage caused by the operation of a domestic vehicle in the territory of another member state, the scope of liability insurance is governed by the legal regulations of that other member state, unless this scope is broader according to this Act or the insurance contract.

(8) Damage caused by the operation of a vehicle to a citizen of a member state during their direct journey between two territories of member states, if the territory through which such person is passing and where the event from which the damage arose occurred is not covered by the jurisdiction of any national insurers' bureau, is governed by the legal regulations of the member state in whose territory the vehicle has its usual base.

p7.text:
(1) The insurer shall not cover

- a) damage suffered by the driver of the vehicle whose operation caused the damage,

- b) damage under § 6 para. 2 letter b) and c) that the insured person is obliged to compensate their spouse or persons who lived with them in a common household at the time of the insured event, with the exception of damage under § 6 para. 2 letter c), if this damage is related to damage under § 6 para. 2 letter a),

- c) damage to the vehicle whose operation caused the damage, as well as to items transported by this vehicle, with the exception of damage caused to an item that a person transported by this vehicle had on or with them at the time of the insured event, to the extent that the insured person is obliged to compensate for the damage,

- d) damage under § 6 para. 2 letter b) and c) arising between vehicles of a road train consisting of a motor vehicle and a trailer, as well as damage to items transported by these vehicles, unless it is damage caused by the operation of another vehicle,

- e) damage arising from the manipulation of cargo of a stationary vehicle,

- f) costs incurred by the provision of medical care, sickness insurance benefits (care), or pensions from pension insurance as a result of bodily injury or death suffered by the driver of the vehicle whose operation caused this damage,

- g) damage caused by the operation of a vehicle during its participation in an organized motor race or competition, with the exception of damage caused during such participation, if the driver is obliged to observe road traffic rules during this race or competition,

- h) damage arising from the operation of a vehicle during a terrorist act or war event, if this operation has a direct connection with this act or event.

(2) If damage was caused to the owner of a vehicle by the operation of their vehicle, which was driven by another person at the time the damage occurred, or to a person who lawfully disposes of the vehicle, whose operation caused damage to this person, as their own, or who lawfully exercises a right for themselves with it, and if another person was driving the vehicle at the time the damage occurred, the insurer is obliged to compensate this owner or this person only for damage under § 6 para. 2 letter a), including reimbursement of costs incurred for care covered by public health insurance and a recourse claim under § 6 para. 4.

(3) In the event of a collision of vehicles owned by the same person, damage caused to this person shall be compensated only if there are different operators of the vehicles involved in the occurrence of the insured event and if this person is not simultaneously the operator of the vehicle on which this damage was caused.

(4) Compensation for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle, to the extent that the insured person is obliged to compensate for this damage and which the injured party has duly proven, cannot be refused or reduced, with the exception of cases specified in this Act.

p8.heading: Duties of the Insured
p8.text:
(1) The insured is obliged to notify the insurer in writing without undue delay that a loss event has occurred, stating the factual circumstances relating to this event, to submit relevant documents, and to proceed in accordance with the insurer's instructions during the investigation of the loss event.

(2) The insured is obliged to inform the insurer in writing without undue delay that

- a) a right to compensation for damage has been asserted against them and to comment on the requested compensation and its amount,

- b) administrative or criminal proceedings have been initiated in connection with the loss event and to promptly inform the insurer of their progress and outcome,

- c) the injured party has asserted a right to compensation for damage in court or before another competent authority, if they become aware of this fact.

(3) The insured is obliged to provide the injured party without undue delay, upon their request, with the data necessary for the injured party to assert their right to performance under § 9 para. 1, but at least

- a) their first name(s) and surname and domicile, name or business name and registered office,

- b) the first name(s) and surname and domicile, name or business name and registered office, and possibly also the place of business of the vehicle owner,

- c) the business name and registered office of the insurer, and possibly also the address of the insurer's branch office in the Czech Republic,

- d) the insurance policy number, and

- e) if it is a vehicle subject to vehicle registration, the vehicle's license plate number by which the damage was caused.

(4) In the event of a traffic accident not subject to notification to the Police of the Czech Republic according to the law regulating road traffic^15c), the insured is obliged to submit a joint record of the traffic accident^15d) to the insurer without undue delay.

(5) If the insurer requests the insured to notify the Police of the Czech Republic of a traffic accident according to paragraph 4, the insurer shall reimburse the insured for the related costs.

p9.heading: Insurance Benefit
p9.text:
(1) The injured party has the right to assert their claim for performance under § 6 with the relevant insurer or with the Office, if it is a claim for performance from the guarantee fund under § 24. When asserting the claim, they are obliged to submit a joint record of the traffic accident^15d), if it is a traffic accident not subject to notification to the Police of the Czech Republic according to the law regulating road traffic^15c).

(2) The insurer's performance is due within 15 days, once the insurer has completed the investigation necessary to ascertain the extent of its obligation to perform or once the insurer has received a final court decision on its obligation to provide insurance benefits.

(3) The insurer is obliged to investigate the loss event without undue delay. Within a period of 3 months from the day on which the authorized person asserted the right to performance from liability insurance, the insurer is obliged to

- a) conclude the investigation of the insured event and inform the injured party of the amount of the insurance benefit according to the individual claims of the injured party, including the method of determining its amount, if the insurer's obligation to perform from liability insurance has not been disputed and the claims of the injured party have been proven, or

- b) provide the injured party with a written explanation regarding those claims asserted by them that were rejected by the insurer or for which the insurer's performance was reduced, or for which the investigation could not be concluded within the stipulated period.

(4) If the insurer has not fulfilled the obligation under paragraph 3, the amount of the insurance benefit to which the insurer's delay relates shall be increased by default interest. The default interest rate shall be set at the discount rate determined by the Czech National Bank, valid on the first day of the delay, increased by 4% per annum. Default interest shall always be determined separately for each commenced month of delay at one-twelfth of the default interest rate, with the default interest rate being updated each month using the discount rate of the Czech National Bank, valid on the first day of the given month of delay. Default interest derived from the discount rate shall apply unless a higher amount is stipulated in the insurance policy.

(5) If criminal proceedings under a special legal regulation^16) or misdemeanor proceedings are not jeopardized, the body active in criminal proceedings or the body conducting misdemeanor proceedings shall provide the insurer or the Office with data on the traffic accident to the extent specified by a special legal regulation,^16a) or shall allow them to inspect the file and make excerpts from it in matters relating to the loss event.

(6) The investigation under paragraph 3 cannot be conditioned by the conclusion of proceedings under paragraph 5, the subject of which is not a decision on compensation for damage. This does not affect the provision of paragraph 2.

p9a.heading: Claims Representative of the Insurer in Another Member State
p9a.text:
(1) An insurer with its registered office in the Czech Republic shall appoint a claims representative in every other Member State. This also applies to an insurer with its registered office in a state other than a Member State (hereinafter referred to as a „third state“), with the exception of those Member States where it has been granted authorisation to operate liability insurance. This claims representative must have, if a natural person, a domicile or place of business, and if a legal person, a registered office in the Member State where they were appointed.

(2) The claims representative under paragraph 1 shall handle claims on behalf and for the account of the insurer, including the final settlement of claims raised by the injured party. For this purpose, they shall collect information necessary to investigate the injured party's claims and take measures for their settlement, if the damage was caused by the operation of a vehicle:

- a) for which the insurer concluded liability insurance in a Member State other than the state where the injured party has their domicile, place of business, or registered office,

- b) which is normally based in a Member State other than the state where the injured party has their domicile, place of business, or registered office, and

- c) if this damage occurred in a Member State other than the state where the injured party has their domicile, place of business, or registered office, or

- d) if this damage occurred in a third state whose bureau of insurers has joined the green card system.

(3) The claims representative under paragraph 1 may also act for multiple insurers. They must be authorised to represent the insurer in relation to the injured party, to settle the injured party's claims in full, and be able to act in the official language of the Member State of the injured party's domicile or registered office. The appointment of a claims representative under paragraph 1 does not exclude the injured party's right to claim compensation for damage directly against the person who caused the damage or against the relevant insurer.

(4) The appointment of a claims representative under paragraph 1 is not the establishment of a branch of the insurer, and the activity of the claims representative is not considered a form of establishment in the territory of another Member State (hereinafter referred to as an „establishment“).

(5) The insurer is obliged to notify the Bureau of the name, or names, and surname and address of domicile, place of business, or trade name of the claims representative under paragraph 1, if a natural person, or the trade name or name and registered office, if a legal person, the state of their activity, and all changes, within 7 days from the date of their appointment or the change. The Bureau shall transmit this information without undue delay to the relevant national information centres of the Member States.

p9b.heading: Claims representative of an insurer from another Member State for the Czech Republic
p9b.text:
(1) An insurer with its registered office in another Member State, which operates liability insurance in the Czech Republic on the basis of the freedom to provide services temporarily, is obliged to appoint its claims representative in the Czech Republic.

(2) The claims representative under paragraph 1 shall handle claims on behalf and for the account of the insurer, including the final settlement of claims raised by the injured party. For this purpose, they shall collect information necessary to investigate the injured party's claims and take measures for their settlement, if the damage was caused by the operation of a vehicle:

- a) for which the insurer concluded liability insurance, if the injured party has their domicile, place of business, or registered office in the Czech Republic, and

- b) if this damage occurred in the territory of a Member State or another state according to § 6 (5) or (6).

(3) The claims representative under paragraph 1 may also act for multiple insurers. They must be authorised to represent the insurer in relation to the injured party, to settle the injured party's legitimate claims in full, and be able to act in the Czech language. The appointment of a claims representative under paragraph 1 does not exclude the injured party's right to claim compensation for damage directly against the person who caused the damage or against the relevant insurer.

(4) The appointment of a claims representative under paragraph 1 is not the establishment of a branch of the insurer, and the activity of the claims representative is not considered an establishment.

(5) The insurer is obliged to notify the Bureau of the name, or names, and surname and address of domicile, place of business, or trade name of the claims representative under paragraph 1, if a natural person, or the trade name or name and registered office, if a legal person, within 7 days from the date of their appointment. The insurer is obliged to notify the Bureau of all changes concerning this claims representative within 7 days from the date of such change.

(6) The claims representative under paragraph 1 represents the insurer under paragraph 1 in relation to the Bureau in matters regulated in § 15 and in § 24 (7) and (9), even if the power of representation under § 18 (10) belongs to another person.

p9c.heading: Claims representative of an insurance company for the Czech Republic
p9c.text:
(1) A claims representative appointed in accordance with European Community law^1) in the Czech Republic by an insurer that has been granted authorisation to operate liability insurance in a Member State other than the Czech Republic and that does not operate liability insurance in the Czech Republic, shall handle, on behalf of and for the account of that insurer, claims events, including compensation for damage caused to the injured party by the operation of a vehicle,

- a) for which the insurer has concluded liability insurance, if the injured party has a permanent residence, place of business or registered office in the Czech Republic,

- b) which is normally based in a Member State other than the Czech Republic, and

- c) if such damage occurred in a Member State other than the Czech Republic, or

- d) if such damage occurred in a third state whose bureau of insurers has joined the green card system.

(2) The claims representative under paragraph 1 shall collect information necessary to investigate the claims of the injured party and shall take measures to settle them, if the damage was caused by the operation of a vehicle, provided that the insurer concluded liability insurance for the operation of that vehicle effective at the time of the claims event. The claims representative must be authorised to represent the insurer in relation to the injured party, to settle the legitimate claims of the injured party in full, and be able to act in the Czech language. The appointment of a claims representative under paragraph 1 does not exclude the right of the injured party to claim compensation directly against the person who caused the damage or against the relevant insurer.

(3) The claims representative under paragraph 1 shall exercise the rights and obligations of the insurer under this Act in relation to the injured party, including representing the insurer before courts in decisions concerning these rights or obligations.

(4) A claims representative under paragraph 1 may also act for multiple insurers. Their appointment does not constitute the establishment of a branch of the insurer, and the activity of the claims representative is not considered an establishment.

p9d.text: If an insurer operates liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic on the basis of the right to establish branches as an insurer with its registered office in another Member State, it is presumed that the duties of the claims representative under § 9c (1) to (3) are performed by the head of that branch in the territory of the Czech Republic, unless the insurer has appointed another person as the claims representative for the liability insurance policies concluded by it for the operation of a vehicle normally based in a Member State other than the Czech Republic. If an insurer operates liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic on the basis of the freedom to temporarily provide services as an insurer under § 9b (1), it is presumed that the duties of the claims representative under § 9c (1) to (3) are performed by the claims representative under § 9b, unless the insurer has appointed another person as the claims representative for the liability insurance policies concluded by it for the operation of a vehicle normally based in a Member State other than the Czech Republic.

p10.heading: Insurer's right to reimbursement of the amount paid
p10.text:
(1) The insurer shall have a right of recourse against the insured for what it has paid on his behalf if it proves that the insured

- a) caused the damage intentionally,

- b) without a worthy reason, failed to fulfil the obligation under the Act regulating road traffic to draw up a joint record of a traffic accident or to report a traffic accident which is an insured event, and as a result, the possibility of a proper investigation by the insurer under § 9 (3) of this Act or the possibility of the insurer to assert this right to compensation for the insurance benefit was hindered or made impossible,

- c) without a worthy reason, left the scene of the traffic accident or otherwise made it impossible to ascertain the actual cause of the traffic accident,

- d) caused damage by operating a vehicle which he used without authorisation,

- e) without a worthy reason, failed to fulfil the obligation under § 8 (1) to (3) and as a result, the possibility of a proper investigation by the insurer under § 9 (3) was hindered or made impossible,

- f) without a worthy reason, as the driver of the vehicle, refused to undergo a test for the presence of alcohol, narcotic or psychotropic substance or a drug marked with a driving prohibition, at the request of a member of the Police of the Czech Republic,

- g) drove a vehicle and was not the holder of the relevant driving licence, with the exception of driving a vehicle by a person who is learning to drive a vehicle or is taking a driving test, and always only under the supervision of an authorised instructor or a driving instructor for individual training,

- h) drove a vehicle at a time when he was prohibited from driving a vehicle,

- i) drove a vehicle under the influence of alcohol, a narcotic or psychotropic substance or a drug marked with a driving prohibition, or

- j) handed over the driving of the vehicle to a person specified in letters g), h), i).

(2) The insurer shall have a right of recourse against the insured for what it has paid on his behalf if it proves that the insured breached a fundamental obligation concerning road traffic by operating a vehicle,

- a) which by its design or technical condition does not meet the requirements for road safety, operating personnel, transported persons and goods, or

- b) whose technical fitness for vehicle operation has not been approved,

and this breach was in causal connection with the occurrence of the damage which the insured is obliged to compensate.

(3) The operator of the vehicle shall be jointly and severally liable with the person under paragraph 1 for the insurer's claim for compensation for the amount paid under paragraph 1 letters g), h), i) and j), unless he proves that he could not influence the actions of that person.

(4) The insurer shall have a right to reimbursement from the policyholder for the amount it has paid due to damage caused by the operation of a vehicle, if its cause was a fact which the insurer could not ascertain when arranging the insurance due to knowingly false or incomplete answers and which was essential for concluding the insurance contract.

(5) If the insured has breached any of the obligations specified in § 8 (1) to (3), the insurer shall be entitled to demand from him reimbursement of costs associated with the investigation of the insured event or other costs incurred by the breach of these obligations. This does not affect the insurer's right to reimbursement of the paid amount under paragraph 1 letter e).

(6) The sum of the demanded reimbursements under paragraphs 1 to 5 must not exceed the benefit paid by the insurer as a result of the insured event to which this right of the insurer relates.

p11.heading: Damage caused by the operation of a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance
p11.text:
(1) Damage caused by the operation of a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance shall be compensated by the Ministry.

(2) The method and scope of compensation for damage in cases under paragraph 1 shall be governed by the Civil Code. A person operating a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance is obliged to notify the Ministry of the facts under § 8 (1) and (2). In such a case, the injured party has a right to compensation for damage against the Ministry. Activities related to the compensation of damage paid by the Ministry may be performed on behalf of the Ministry by an insurer authorised by it, based on an agreement.

(3) If damage caused by the operation of a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance occurs in the territory of a state listed in the list of states under § 6 (4), this damage shall be compensated by the Bureau or the insurer specified in paragraph 2. The Ministry shall reimburse the person who compensated the damage under the first sentence for the amount of the paid insurance benefit, including costs associated with the settlement of the insured event.

(4) If the Ministry has compensated damage caused by the operation of a vehicle, which is to be compensated by a person operating a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance, the Ministry has a right of recourse against the natural person who caused the damage, for what it has paid on behalf of that person, under the conditions of § 10 (1). If the damage was caused in the performance of work duties, the Ministry may assert a right to reimbursement of the paid benefit to the extent that the employer could assert it against the employee under a special legal regulation.^18) This limitation does not apply if the damage was caused intentionally, by unauthorised use of the vehicle, after consuming an alcoholic beverage or under the influence of an intoxicating substance.

Termination of liability insurance

p12.text:
(1) Liability insurance under this Act shall terminate

- a) on the day on which the policyholder, their heir, legal successor or the owner of the vehicle, if different from the policyholder, notified the insurer of a change of ownership of a domestic vehicle,

- b) on the day on which the vehicle, which is not subject to vehicle registration under the Act regulating the conditions for operating vehicles on roads, ceased to exist,

- c) on the day of decommissioning a domestic vehicle from operation under the Act on the conditions for operating vehicles on roads, unless the insurer and the policyholder have agreed to suspend it,

- d) by theft of the vehicle; if the time of the vehicle's theft cannot be precisely determined, the vehicle shall be considered stolen as soon as the Police of the Czech Republic received notification of the vehicle's theft,

- e) on the day following the fruitless expiry of the period set by the insurer in a reminder for payment of the insurance premium or part thereof, delivered to the policyholder; this period must not be shorter than 1 month and the insurer's reminder must contain a warning about the termination of liability insurance in case of non-payment of the outstanding premium, and the insurer shall simultaneously inform the person specified in the insurance contract as the owner of the domestic vehicle, if this person is different from the policyholder, about this reminder; the period set by the insurer in the reminder for payment of the insurance premium or part thereof may be extended by agreement before its expiry,

- f) by termination according to a special legal regulation,^19)

- g) by agreement,

- h) by the expiry of the period for which the liability insurance was concluded.

(2) The policyholder is obliged to notify the insurer without undue delay of the facts stated in paragraph 1 letters a) to d).

(3) If a vehicle subject to vehicle registration ceases to exist, the day of termination of liability insurance shall be the day of the entry of its cessation in the road vehicle register, with the exception of cases where the person authorized to request the entry of the vehicle's cessation could not submit such a request due to reasons beyond their control and if they did so without delay once these obstacles ceased to exist. In such a case, the day of termination of liability insurance shall be the day on which an irreversible change occurred making its operation impossible.

p13.text:
(1) After the termination of liability insurance,

- a) the policyholder is obliged to hand over the green card to the insurer without undue delay, if one was issued,

- b) the insurer is obliged, based on a written request from the policyholder, to issue to them, within 15 days from the date of its delivery, a confirmation of the duration of the liability insurance and its claims history, the particulars of which shall be stipulated by the Ministry by decree; within the same period, the insurer is obliged to issue this confirmation at the policyholder's request at any time during the insurance period, and the insurer has the same obligation if the policyholder requests a confirmation of the return of the green card to the insurer.

 The particulars of this confirmation shall be stipulated by the Ministry by decree.

(2) If the liability insurance terminates before the expiry of the period for which the insurance was concluded, the insurer is entitled to the insurance premium until the end of the calendar month in which the liability insurance terminated. The remaining part of the paid insurance premium shall be returned by the insurer. If an insured event occurred during the period until the termination of the liability insurance, the insurer is entitled to the insurance premium according to the first sentence; the obligation to return the remaining part of the insurance premium arises only if no obligation to pay arises for the insurer from this insured event.

(3) If the policyholder has not fulfilled the obligation according to paragraph 1 letter a), the insurer is not obliged to return the insurance premium to the policyholder according to paragraph 2 or to issue the confirmation according to paragraph 1 letter b) until this obligation is fulfilled.

(4) The policyholder's obligation according to paragraph 1 letter a) also applies in the event of suspension of liability insurance.

p14.heading: Border insurance
p14.text:
(1) The driver of a foreign vehicle, with the exception of a driver who holds a valid green card issued in a foreign state, and with the exception of a driver whose liability insurance in the territory of all Member States is guaranteed by the bureau of insurers of a foreign state, is obliged to conclude an insurance contract with the Bureau for liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of a foreign vehicle (hereinafter referred to as "border insurance"). Border insurance is concluded upon entry of a foreign vehicle into the territory of the Czech Republic by paying the insurance premium, for a minimum period of 15 days. Without border insurance, the Police of the Czech Republic must not allow such a vehicle to be driven in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(2) Border insurance covers only insured events that occurred in the territory of the Czech Republic and in the territory of another Member State. Activities related to the conclusion of border insurance may be performed on behalf of the Bureau by a person authorized by it on the basis of a contract.

(3) The Bureau shall issue a green card to the driver of the vehicle as proof of border insurance as soon as the insurance premium has been paid.

(4) If a foreign vehicle, whose operation in the territory of the Czech Republic is conditional on border insurance, is operated in the territory of the Czech Republic without concluding this insurance, the driver of this vehicle is obliged to pay the Bureau an insurance premium corresponding to three times the insurance premium that should have been paid for the period of stay of this vehicle in the territory of the Czech Republic, but at least CZK 10,000. The same applies if such a foreign vehicle is operated in the territory of the Czech Republic without border insurance after the expiry of the validity period of the green card.

(5) If the driver of a foreign vehicle, whose operation in the territory of the Czech Republic is conditional on border insurance, does not prove, upon the departure of this vehicle from the territory of the Czech Republic, with a document on border insurance concluded for the entire period of stay of this vehicle in the territory of the Czech Republic or for the period of this stay after the expiry of the validity period of the green card, a member of the Police of the Czech Republic shall not allow further driving of the foreign vehicle until the obligation under paragraph 4 is fulfilled.

(6) The paid insurance premium for border insurance shall not be refunded.

p15.heading: Record of liability insurance
p15.text:
(1) The insurer shall provide the Office with data on the inception, period of suspension, change, and termination of liability insurance, at least to the extent of the data stipulated by the Ministry in a decree, no later than 1 month from the date of the relevant event.

(2) The Office shall provide the data pursuant to paragraph 1 concerning vehicles subject to vehicle registration to the Ministry of Transport.

(3) The Ministry of Transport shall compare the data pursuant to paragraph 2 with the data maintained by it and shall inform the Office of the result of the comparison and of data on vehicles and their owners or operators that were not provided by the Office, to the extent of the data pursuant to paragraph 12 maintained in the road vehicle register. The Office shall have the right to request the provision of individual data maintained in the road vehicle register if there are reasons for doing so arising from its scope of activity under this Act.

(4) Data maintained by the Office concerning vehicles and their owners or operators may be provided by the Office to the insurer.

(5) If the liability insurance for a vehicle, the operation of which is conditional on liability insurance, terminates, the owner of a vehicle subject to vehicle registration shall be obliged to request the deregistration of the vehicle from operation in accordance with the act regulating the conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads, unless the vehicle is recorded in the road vehicle register as defunct or exported to another state.

(6) If the Office, based on a comparison of data pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 3, ascertains that after the termination of liability insurance, a new insurance contract was not concluded pursuant to paragraph 5, it shall notify this fact without undue delay to the registration authority competent according to the registration of the domestic vehicle, via the road vehicle register. Based on this notification, the registration authority shall verify compliance with the obligation; if it ascertains non-compliance with this obligation, it shall proceed in accordance with the act regulating the conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads^20).

(7) The data pursuant to paragraphs 2, 3, and 6 shall be provided at least once a month.

(8) The data shall be provided in a manner enabling remote access.

(9) Insurers shall provide the Office with data on claims and insured events, including personal data, for processing for the purposes of § 3b (2), § 18 (2) (f) and (g) of this Act and the Insurance Act^24). The provisions of paragraphs 1, 4, 7, and 8 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(10) The processing of personal data by the insurer or the Office in connection with the fulfillment of obligations under this Act shall be considered processing necessary for compliance with a legal obligation of the controller under the act regulating the protection of personal data^25).

(11) If the result of administrative proceedings pursuant to paragraph 6 was the deregistration of the vehicle from operation in accordance with the Act on the Conditions for the Operation of Vehicles on Roads, the relevant registration authority shall communicate this fact to the Office via the operation in accordance with the Act on the Conditions for the Operation of Vehicles on Roads in a manner enabling remote access, without undue delay after the conclusion of the relevant proceedings.

(12) When fulfilling the obligations pursuant to paragraph 3, the Ministry of Transport shall provide the Office from the road vehicle register, for each vehicle about whose termination of liability insurance the Office informed the Ministry of Transport pursuant to paragraph 2, the following data:

- a) first name(s) and surname, birth number, and place of residence in the case of a natural person, or name, identification number, and registered office in the case of a legal entity as the registered owner of this vehicle and its registered operator, if different from the owner,

- b) the state registration plate of the road motor vehicle or trailer,

- c) the technical certificate number of the road motor vehicle or trailer,

- d) the type and category of the road motor vehicle or trailer,

- e) the manufacturer of the road motor vehicle or trailer, the make (trade name specified by the manufacturer), vehicle type, commercial designation,

- f) the identification number of the road motor vehicle or trailer (VIN), if not available, then the chassis production number of the road motor vehicle or trailer,

- g) the type, power, and displacement of the engine,

- h) the maximum technically permissible mass, maximum authorized mass, and operating mass of the road motor vehicle,

- i) the date of first registration of the road motor vehicle or trailer and

- j) the date of deregistration of the vehicle from operation or the date of termination of the vehicle in accordance with the Act on the Conditions for the Operation of Vehicles on Roads.

p15a.heading: Information Centre of the Office
p15a.text:
(1) For the purpose of providing information necessary for the assertion of claims by injured parties for compensation for damage from liability insurance, the Bureau operates an information centre, through which it ascertains and stores data on

- a) national registration plates of domestic vehicles,

- b) the insurer, policy numbers relating to liability insurance for the operation of domestic vehicles, and the start and end dates of the insurance's validity,

- c) green card numbers, policy numbers relating to border insurance,

- d) insurers and their appointed claims representatives pursuant to § 9a,

- e) claims representatives pursuant to § 9b and 9c.

(2) The Bureau ascertains information pursuant to paragraph 1 letter a), concerning domestic vehicles, through the road vehicle register (§ 15 para 3). The Ministry collects information pursuant to paragraph 1 letters a) and c) concerning vehicles with an exemption from liability insurance pursuant to § 5 para 1 letters b) and c). Upon the Bureau's request, the Ministry is obliged to inform it whether it is a vehicle whose owner or operator is subject to this exemption.

(3) Information pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2 must be stored for at least 7 years from the date of permanent deregistration of the vehicle from the road vehicle register or from the date of termination of the insurance policy, so that the injured party can always obtain information from the Bureau within 7 years from the date of the insured event concerning

- a) the business name and registered office of the insurer who concluded the liability insurance policy for the operation of the vehicle that caused the damage to the injured party,

- b) the policy number of the liability insurance concluded by the insurer pursuant to letter a),

- c) the claims representative pursuant to § 9a or § 9b,

- d) the owner of the vehicle or its operator, if different from its owner, and

- e) the place where the injured party should turn in case the damage was caused by the operation of a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance.

(4) The Bureau ascertains information concerning vehicles with usual situs in a Member State other than the Czech Republic, and information on claims representatives pursuant to § 9c, through the information centres of these Member States.

Offences

p16.heading: Offences by natural persons, legal entities, and self-employed natural persons
p16.text:
(1) A natural person, legal entity, or self-employed natural person commits an offence by breaching an obligation pursuant to § 1 para 2 letter b).

(2) A natural person, as a driver of a vehicle, commits an offence by failing to present a green card in contravention of § 17 para 1.

(3) For an offence pursuant to paragraph 1, a fine of 5,000 CZK to 40,000 CZK may be imposed, and for an offence pursuant to paragraph 2, a fine of 1,500 CZK to 3,000 CZK.

(4) By on-the-spot fine, a fine of up to 1,500 CZK may be imposed for an offence pursuant to paragraph 2.

p16b.heading: Joint Provisions
p16b.text:
(1) Offences under this Act are dealt with by the municipal authority of a municipality with extended powers, and in the Capital City of Prague, by its municipal district. Offences under § 16 para 2 may be dealt with by the Police of the Czech Republic by on-the-spot fine. In case of detection of a breach of § 1 para 2, the authority pursuant to the first sentence shall inform the Bureau of this fact without undue delay, in a manner allowing remote access. The information shall include data on the vehicle and its owner.

(2) The local jurisdiction of the administrative authority for dealing with an offence by a natural person under § 16 para 1 is governed by the domicile of the natural person suspected of committing it, and the local jurisdiction for dealing with an offence by a legal entity or self-employed natural person under § 16 para 1 is governed by the registered office of the legal entity or self-employed natural person suspected of committing the offence.

(3) Fines are enforced and collected by the authority that imposed them. Fines imposed by on-the-spot fine by the Police of the Czech Republic that are not paid on the spot are enforced by the relevant tax administrator.

p17.heading: Control of liability insurance
p17.text:
(1) When operating a vehicle on a road, its driver is obliged to carry a green card or a border insurance document and to present it to a member of the Police of the Czech Republic upon request. This does not apply to drivers of vehicles with an exemption from liability insurance and drivers of foreign vehicles whose liability insurance is guaranteed by the bureau of insurers of a foreign state.

(2) The Police of the Czech Republic carries out control of liability insurance in the event of a foreign vehicle entering the territory of the Czech Republic. Unless otherwise stipulated, the Police of the Czech Republic does not carry out control of liability insurance in the event of a foreign vehicle with usual situs in the territory of another Member State entering the territory of the Czech Republic, and a vehicle with usual situs in the territory of a non-Member State, if such vehicle enters the territory of the Czech Republic from the territory of another Member State.

(3) The Police of the Czech Republic is authorized to carry out control of liability insurance upon every entry into the territory of the Czech Republic of a vehicle with usual situs in the territory of another Member State, which is listed in the list of vehicles that the Member State has submitted, in accordance with European Community law,^20b) to the Ministry and the Commission of the European Communities.

(4) The Police of the Czech Republic carries out random control of liability insurance, which must not be discriminatory, is carried out as part of border police control, and is not exclusively focused only on the control of liability insurance of a vehicle that has its usual situs in the territory of a state other than the Czech Republic and that enters the territory of the Czech Republic from another Member State.

(5) The Ministry shall transmit the list of vehicles pursuant to paragraph 3 and all its changes without undue delay after its receipt to the Bureau and the Police of the Czech Republic. The Ministry shall notify other Member States and the Commission of the European Communities of the list of persons with an exemption from liability insurance^20b) (§ 5 para 2).

Part Three

The Bureau

p18.text:
(1) The Czech Insurers' Bureau is hereby established as a professional organization of insurers. The Bureau is a legal entity with its registered office in Prague, authorized to carry out activities to the extent stipulated by this Act. The provisions of the Act regulating insurance shall apply mutatis mutandis to the activities of the Bureau. The Bureau shall be registered in the commercial register.

(2) The Bureau shall

- a) administer the Guarantee Fund and the Loss Prevention Fund,

- b) arrange frontier insurance and operate an information centre,

- c) ensure the fulfilment of tasks related to its membership in the Council of Bureaux,

- d) conclude agreements with insurers' bureaux of foreign states and guarantee funds of foreign states, information centres and bodies authorized in other member states to handle requests for substitute performance, and ensure the tasks arising from these agreements,

- e) cooperate with state authorities in matters concerning liability insurance,

- f) maintain records and statistics for the purposes of liability insurance,

- g) participate in preventing the occurrence of loss events in road traffic and in preventing insurance fraud in insurance related to vehicle operation,

- h) process data on traffic accidents from the Police of the Czech Republic for its own needs, the needs of its members or insurers' bureaux of foreign states and their member insurance companies, in a manner enabling remote access to the data,

- i) enable an insurance company to use the information system for utilizing data from public administration information systems in accordance with the Act regulating insurance^28) and

- j) maintain and administer the register of health insurance of foreigners and provide data therefrom in accordance with the Act regulating the stay of foreigners in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(3) Membership in the Bureau arises on the day the decision of the Czech National Bank, by which an insurer was granted authorization to operate liability insurance, becomes final, or on the day an insurer from another member state commences activity in this insurance class in the territory of the Czech Republic. An insurer to whom authorization to operate liability insurance has been granted by the Czech National Bank is obliged to notify the Bureau in writing without undue delay of the commencement of membership. An insurer with its registered office in another member state is obliged to notify the Bureau in writing without undue delay, in the event of operating liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic, of

- a) the anticipated date of commencement of activity in liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic through a branch established in the territory of the Czech Republic and the first name(s), surname, date of birth and address for service of the person authorized to act on behalf of the insurer with the Bureau, if it is a natural person, and the business name or name and registered office, if it is a legal entity,

- b) the anticipated date of commencement of activity in liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic on the basis of the freedom to provide services temporarily and the first name(s), surname, date of birth and address for service of the person authorized to act on behalf of the insurer with the Bureau, if it is a natural person, and the business name or name and registered office, if it is a legal entity,

- c) the actual date of commencement or the date of termination of activity in liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic, no later than on this date,

- d) a change in the person with authorization to act vis-à-vis the Bureau, no later than on the date this change becomes effective.

(4) Membership in the Bureau ceases on the day the decision of the Czech National Bank, by which an insurer's authorization to operate liability insurance was withdrawn, becomes final. For an insurer with its registered office in another member state who operates liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic, membership in the Bureau ceases on the day of termination of this activity.

(5) A member of the Bureau is obliged to pay contributions to the Bureau. Contributions may only be used to ensure the scope of activity of the Bureau.

(6) A member of the Bureau guarantees its debts for which the Bureau does not have adequate assets, in the same proportion as its share in the total amount of contributions of all members of the Bureau. The Bureau provides information on its assets and liabilities to its members and to the Czech National Bank.

(7) A member of the Bureau shall take into account in its own calculation of the solvency capital requirement according to the Act regulating insurance the risk of a change in the amount of the guarantee according to paragraph 6. For this purpose, the Bureau shall assess, according to the Act regulating insurance, all risks to which it is exposed, and shall provide this calculation to the members of the Bureau and to the Czech National Bank.

(8) The Czech National Bank shall without undue delay communicate to the Bureau the facts known to it according to paragraphs 3 and 4. If the Czech National Bank, according to the Act regulating insurance, suspends an insurer's authorization to conclude insurance contracts in liability insurance or orders an insurer to transfer the insurance portfolio of liability insurance, it shall notify the Bureau of this fact without undue delay.

(9) If the Bureau ascertains a breach of an obligation towards the Bureau stipulated by this Act by its member, it is obliged to communicate such fact to the Czech National Bank without undue delay.

(10) Until the Bureau receives written notification from an insurer with its registered office outside the territory of the Czech Republic regarding the authorization of another person to act, the following shall act with the Bureau on behalf of this insurer

- a) the head of the branch established in the territory of the Czech Republic, if liability insurance is operated through it, or

- b) the claims representative for the Czech Republic according to § 9b, if liability insurance is operated on the basis of the freedom to provide services temporarily.

(11) The keeping of accounts of the Bureau shall be governed by a special legal regulation governing the keeping of accounts for insurance companies.^22a) The financial statements of the Bureau must be audited by an auditor.^22b)

(12) For the fulfilment of tasks according to this Act, the Bureau shall use data from the basic register of inhabitants, data from the information system of population records, data from the information system of foreigners, data from the information system of identity card records or data from the information system of travel document records to the extent and under the conditions according to the Act regulating insurance, similarly to an insurance company.

p18a.heading: Information obligations of the Bureau
p18a.text:
(1) The Bureau shall, at the request of an injured party with a domicile or registered office in the Czech Republic, provide them with:

- a) the first name(s), surname, and address of domicile or place of business, or the business name, or the name and registered office of the owner or customary driver or registered keeper of the vehicle whose operation caused the damage to the injured party,

- b) the business name and address of the insurer with whom liability for the operation of the vehicle whose operation caused the damage to the injured party was insured,

- c) the number of the insurance contract under which liability for the operation of the vehicle whose operation caused the damage to the injured party was insured,

- d) the first name(s), surname, and address of domicile or place of business, or the name and registered office of the claims representative pursuant to § 9b or § 9c,

if the damaging event occurred in a Member State due to the operation of a vehicle with a habitual situs in a Member State; in the case of a claims representative pursuant to § 9b, also in the territory of a state listed in § 6 para. 5 or 6.

(2) The Bureau shall, at the request of an injured party with a registered office or domicile in a Member State other than the Czech Republic, provide them with similar information pursuant to paragraph 1, if:

- a) the damaging event occurred in the territory of the Czech Republic, or

- b) the vehicle whose operation caused the damage has its habitual situs in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(3) An injured party requesting the provision of information pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2 shall, in their request, inform the Bureau of:

- a) the date and place of the damaging event, including the identification of the Member State in whose territory the damaging event occurred, and

- b) details of the registration plate of the vehicle whose operation caused the damage, and the state in which the registration plate was issued, if known to the injured party.

(4) The Bureau shall provide information concerning a vehicle with a habitual situs in the territory of the Czech Republic, at the request of the information centre of another Member State, in whose territory the injured party has their domicile or registered office or in whose territory the damaging event occurred.

(5) Paragraphs 1 to 3 shall also apply in the case of an injured party with a domicile or registered office in a non-Member State, if in that state the same information would be provided to an injured party with a domicile or registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic.

p19.heading: Bodies of the Bureau
p19.text:
The bodies of the Bureau are:

- a) the General Meeting of Members,

- b) the Board of Directors,

- c) the Supervisory Commission,

- d) the Executive Director.

p20.heading: General Meeting of Members
p20.text:
(1) The supreme body of the Bureau is the General Meeting of Members. Every member of the Bureau has the right to participate in the General Meeting of Members. The General Meeting of Members is convened by the Board of Directors at least once every 12 months. The Board of Directors shall convene the General Meeting of Members to be held no later than 30 days from the date on which the Supervisory Commission or at least one third of the members of the Bureau request it. An authorised representative of the Czech National Bank is entitled to attend every meeting of the General Meeting of Members; the representative of the Czech National Bank must be granted the floor upon their request.

(2) The General Meeting of Members shall have a quorum if more than half of the members of the Bureau who operate liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic otherwise than by way of free provision of services are present. If the General Meeting of Members does not have a quorum, the Board of Directors shall convene a new General Meeting of Members to be held no later than 30 days from the date of the preceding General Meeting of Members; such a meeting shall have a quorum if at least one third of the members of the Bureau who operate liability insurance in the territory of the Czech Republic otherwise than by way of free provision of services are present. Each member has one vote. The General Meeting of Members shall decide by a majority of the votes of the members present.

(3) The General Meeting of Members shall be responsible for:

- a) electing and recalling members of the Board of Directors and the Supervisory Commission,

- b) determining the amount of annual and extraordinary contributions of members,

- c) approving the budget of the Bureau,

- d) repealing or amending decisions of the Board of Directors,

- e) approving the financial statements of the Bureau,

- f) deciding on matters it reserves for itself,

- g) adopting the Statute of the Bureau after its prior approval by the Czech National Bank,

- h) appointing and recalling representatives of the Bureau in the Commission for the creation of damage prevention programmes and for the distribution of funds from the damage prevention fund pursuant to § 23b (hereinafter referred to as the „Commission“).

(4) The contribution of members pursuant to paragraph 3 letter b) shall be determined as a fixed amount per vehicle specified in the insurance contract or as a percentage of the prescribed insurance premium.

p21.heading: Board of Directors
p21.text:
(1) The Board of Directors is the statutory body of the Bureau, consisting of 5 members. Each member of the Board of Directors acts externally on behalf of the Bureau.

(2) The Board of Directors shall be responsible for:

- a) electing and recalling the chairman and vice-chairman of the Board of Directors from among its members,

- b) appointing and recalling the executive director and their deputy,

- c) managing the assets of the Bureau,

- d) approving the internal organisational regulations of the Bureau and their amendments,

- e) approving the establishment or abolition of sections of the Bureau,

- f) ensuring publishing, documentation, and other activities related to the scope of the Bureau's activities,

- g) deciding on the scope of the executive director's authority in the administration of funds from the damage prevention fund, on subsidies from the Bureau's funds to this fund, and, upon the proposal of the Commission, on internal regulations for providing subsidies and loans from this fund.

(3) The Board of Directors shall decide by a majority of its members, usually meeting once a month; meetings shall be convened by its chairman or vice-chairman.

p22.heading: Supervisory Commission
p22.text:
(1) The Supervisory Commission is the supervisory body of the Office. The Supervisory Commission oversees the exercise of the Administrative Board's powers, the performance of the Office's activities and its financial management, and whether members of the Office pay contributions within the stipulated period and amount.

(2) The Supervisory Commission has 3 members and elects and removes its chairman from among its members. A member of the Supervisory Commission may not simultaneously be a member of the Administrative Board, be in an employment relationship or a relationship equivalent thereto with the Office, or be a statutory body or its member, or a member of a supervisory body, or a procurator of a member of the Office.

(3) For the purpose of exercising supervision, the Office must provide the members of the Supervisory Commission with the necessary documents and truthful and complete information about the ascertained facts. The members of the Office have the same obligations if the subject of the supervision is the amount of the contribution paid by that member of the Office.

(4) The Supervisory Commission notifies its findings to the Administrative Board, which is obliged to discuss them and inform the Czech National Bank about the discussion and the measures adopted.

p23.heading: Executive Director
p23.text: The Executive Director is authorized to take legal action to ensure the performance of the Office's powers, and to adopt all measures and decisions necessary to ensure the proper functioning of the Office between meetings of the Administrative Board, unless the Administrative Board determines otherwise.

p23a.heading: Loss Prevention Fund
p23a.text:
(1) The Office establishes a loss prevention fund, which shall be used for the prevention of losses arising from the operation of vehicles. The loss prevention fund is formed from levies from received premiums according to paragraph 2, from yields from investing temporarily free funds of this fund and other funds according to § 21 (2) (g) for the preceding calendar year and a levy according to § 24c, in the given calendar year.

(2) A member of the Office is obliged to contribute to the loss prevention fund at least 3% of the annual received premium from liability insurance for each calendar year. The member of the Office is obliged to pay the contribution to the loss prevention fund to the Office in quarterly installments, with the installment for the 1st quarter of each calendar year being due by April 30, for the 2nd quarter by July 31, for the 3rd quarter by October 31, and for the 4th quarter by January 31 of the immediately following calendar year.

(3) The funds of the loss prevention fund shall be used only to cover costs associated with

- a) the acquisition of technology or material resources necessary for the activities of the basic components of the integrated rescue system and other components of the integrated rescue system providing planned assistance on request in the area of prevention and avoidance of losses from vehicle operation,

- b) the adaptation of technologies and the operation of operational and information centers of the fire and rescue service in connection with providing necessary assistance to motorists,

- c) the implementation of projects focused on road safety according to program priorities approved by the government,

- d) the implementation of programs according to § 23b (4) (b),

- e) the administrative and organizational securing of the Commission's activities, including securing expert assessment of applications for the provision of funds from the loss prevention fund according to letter c) or d) and control of their drawing according to § 23b (4) (f).

(4) From the funds of the loss prevention fund, at least 60% shall be provided to the fire and rescue service for the coverage of costs according to paragraph 3 (a) or (b) in the given calendar year, and of that, at least 20% to the units of voluntary fire brigades of municipalities. Furthermore, from the funds of the loss prevention fund, at least 15% shall be provided to other components of the integrated rescue system and at least 15% for the implementation of projects and programs according to paragraph 3 (c) or (d) in the given calendar year. From the funds of the loss prevention fund, a maximum of 2% shall be provided for the coverage of costs according to paragraph 3 (e) in the given calendar year.
