(5) The recipient of funds from the loss prevention fund is obliged to provide the Commission, upon its request, with all information and documents concerning the use of financial resources from this fund and to enable it to carry out a control of the use of funds from the fund. In the event of failure to prove the use of these funds or a breach of the purpose of their use, the recipient is obliged to return these funds to the loss prevention fund.

(6) If the funds according to paragraph 5 have not been returned within the period set by the Commission, their recipient is obliged to pay default interest and costs associated with their recovery. Funds not returned within the stipulated period shall be recovered by the Office, including interest and associated costs.

p23b.heading: Commission
p23b.text:
(1) A Commission is hereby established as an authority of the Office for the creation of damage prevention programs and for the distribution of funds from the damage prevention fund.

(2) The Commission has 9 members and is composed of two representatives of the Fire and Rescue Service, appointed and removed by the Director General of the Fire and Rescue Service, one representative of the Police of the Czech Republic, appointed and removed by the Police President, one representative appointed and removed by the Minister of the Interior, one representative appointed and removed by the Minister of Health, one representative appointed and removed by the Minister of Transport, and three representatives of the Office pursuant to § 20 para. 3 letter h). The Commission elects and removes a chairperson and a deputy chairperson from among its members. The activities of the Commission are managed by the chairperson or, in his/her absence, by the deputy chairperson. The Commission has a quorum if at least two-thirds of its members are present and decides by a majority of votes of the members present.

(3) The performance of the function of a member of the Commission is considered as another act in the public interest. The employer is obliged to provide the member of the Commission with time off work to the extent necessary for the performance of his/her activities.

(4) The Commission shall

- a) prepare a proposal for the distribution of funds from the damage prevention fund for the following calendar year,

- b) propose prevention programs in the area of damage from vehicle operation,

- c) set evaluation criteria for proposals pursuant to letter a),

- d) approve internal regulations concerning the activities of the Commission,

- e) propose to the administrative board the scope of authority of the executive director in the area of administration of damage prevention fund resources, including regulations pursuant to § 21 para. 2 letter g),

- f) carry out control of the drawing of funds from the damage prevention fund.

(5) The Commission shall submit the proposal pursuant to paragraph 4 letter a) to the administrative board, the Ministry of the Interior, and the Ministry of Transport. The proposal shall be approved if, within 30 days from the date of its delivery, none of the authorities pursuant to the first sentence expresses disagreement due to a conflict between the designation of the damage prevention fund resources and its designation under this Act. In the event of disagreement, the Commission shall submit an amended proposal to these authorities within 30 days from the date of receipt of the disagreement. If at least two of these authorities approve the amended proposal, the amended proposal shall be considered approved.

(6) The Commission shall prepare the annual report of the damage prevention fund for the previous calendar year by June 30 and shall simultaneously publish it in a manner enabling remote access. The annual report must be verified by an independent auditor and shall contain information on the activities of the Commission pursuant to paragraph 4 and on the use of funds from the damage prevention fund in the year for which the financial report is prepared. The costs of the independent auditor for verifying the annual report shall be covered from the damage prevention fund.

p24.heading: Guarantee Fund
p24.text:
(1) The Guarantee Fund shall be formed from insurance premiums for frontier insurance pursuant to § 14, from contributions of insurers pursuant to § 18 (5), from received reimbursements pursuant to § 24 (7) and (9), and from received contributions pursuant to § 4.

(2) The Bureau shall provide the injured party from the Guarantee Fund with

- a) compensation for damage caused by the operation of an unidentified vehicle, which an unidentified person is obliged to compensate; compensation for damage to property or lost profit incurred in connection with property damage shall be provided by the Bureau only if, simultaneously with such damage, the injured party suffered serious health impairment and if the damage pursuant to § 6 (2) (b) or lost profit exceeded CZK 10,000,

- b) compensation for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle, which a person without liability insurance is obliged to compensate, if the operation of this vehicle was conditional on liability insurance under this Act,

- c) compensation for damage caused by the operation of a domestic vehicle, which a person whose liability for such damage is insured with an insurer who, due to its insolvency, cannot compensate for such damage, is obliged to compensate,

- d) compensation for damage caused by the operation of a foreign vehicle, which a person whose liability for such damage is insured by frontier insurance, is obliged to compensate,

- e) compensation for damage caused by the operation of a foreign vehicle, the driver of which is not obliged to conclude frontier insurance when operating this vehicle in the territory of the Czech Republic,

- f) substitute performance pursuant to § 24a and 24b, to the extent to which the Bureau is obliged to perform pursuant to letters a) to c),

- g) compensation for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle that was sent from another Member State to the Czech Republic as the country of destination, if liability insurance under this Act was not concluded for this vehicle and the insured event occurred within 30 days from the date on which the buyer took over the vehicle, even if the vehicle was not registered in the Czech Republic.

(3) Serious health impairment pursuant to paragraph 2 (a) shall mean death or severe health impairment, or health impairment of a permanent nature that hinders the injured party's social integration.

(4) The injured party has the right to claim compensation pursuant to paragraph 2 (b), (c) and (g) against the Bureau under the same conditions under which they could claim compensation against the insurer (§ 9). The Bureau's claim for reimbursement for performance pursuant to paragraph 2 (b), (c) and (g) shall be considered a claim that is deemed to have been filed pursuant to a special legal regulation^22c).

(5) A condition for the injured party's right to compensation from the Guarantee Fund pursuant to paragraph 2 (a) is the investigation of the insured event by the Police of the Czech Republic.

(6) The Bureau shall provide the injured party with compensation pursuant to paragraph 2 under the same conditions as the insurer provides insurance benefits (§ 6 (2) and (5), § 7 to 10).

(7) If a dispute arises between the Bureau and the insurer, or the Ministry in cases specified in § 11, as to who is to compensate the injured party, the Bureau shall provide the injured party with reimbursement for the proven damage. If further investigation reveals that the performance should have been provided in whole or in part by the insurer, or the Ministry, the insurer, or the Ministry, is obliged to reimburse the Bureau for the provided performance.

(8) The maximum limit for performance pursuant to paragraph 2 (a) to (c) and (g) for one insured event shall be the limits of insurance benefits specified in § 3a (2).

(9) By paying compensation from the Guarantee Fund, the Bureau acquires the right to reimbursement of one third of what it paid to the injured party pursuant to paragraph 2 (a), (b) and (g), including the costs of another person authorized by the Bureau to handle the case and provide compensation to the injured party or assert this right, up to a maximum of CZK 300,000. The operator and the driver of the vehicle whose operation caused the damage shall be joint and several debtors with respect to the Bureau's claim for reimbursement pursuant to the first sentence; the driver only if they are obliged to compensate for the damage. Under the conditions pursuant to § 10, the Bureau has the right pursuant to the first and second sentences to reimbursement of what it paid to the injured party, including costs pursuant to the first sentence, without limitation. If the operator of the vehicle cannot be determined, the owner of the vehicle shall be a joint and several debtor with respect to the Bureau's claim. This does not apply to the owner of a vehicle whose liability insurance ceased to exist pursuant to § 12 (1) (d). The Bureau is obliged to claim from the relevant Guarantee Fund, or from the relevant Bureau of Insurers, reimbursement of what it paid to the injured party pursuant to paragraph 2 (e) or in the form of substitute performance pursuant to § 24a (1) (c) and § 24b.

(10) No compensation shall be provided from the Guarantee Fund for

- a) damage caused by the operation of a vehicle with an exemption from liability insurance, unless it is a reimbursement pursuant to paragraph 7,

- b) damages pursuant to § 7,

- c) costs incurred by the provision of medical care, sickness insurance benefits (care) or pensions from pension insurance as a result of health impairment or death caused by the operation of an unidentified vehicle, which an unidentified person is obliged to compensate.

(11) If the Bureau entrusts a member of the Bureau, an independent claims adjuster or another person with handling a case pursuant to paragraph 2 or 9, it shall provide them with all data for its handling, including necessary personal data.

Substitute performance from the Guarantee Fund

p24a.text:
(1) The Office shall provide substitute performance from the Guarantee Fund if

- a) the insurer failed to fulfil its obligation within the period specified in § 9 (3),

- b) an insurer with its registered office in a Member State other than the Czech Republic has not appointed a claims representative in the Czech Republic pursuant to § 9b, or

- c) the insurance company has not appointed a claims representative in the Czech Republic pursuant to § 9c.

(2) Substitute performance pursuant to paragraph 1 (b) shall be provided to the injured party with a domicile or registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic in the event of a loss event caused by the operation of a vehicle, if

- a) the insurance contract for liability insurance arising from its operation was concluded with an insurer operating liability insurance on the basis of the right to temporarily provide services,

- b) it is a vehicle with its usual place of garaging in the Czech Republic, and

- c) the loss event occurred in the territory of a Member State, or in the territory of another state pursuant to § 6 (5) or (6).

(3) Substitute performance pursuant to paragraph 1 (c) shall be provided to the injured party with a domicile or registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic in the event of a loss event caused by the operation of a vehicle, if

- a) the insurance contract for liability insurance arising from its operation was concluded with an insurance company pursuant to § 9c,

- b) it is a vehicle with its usual place of garaging in a Member State other than the Czech Republic, and

- c) the loss event occurred in the territory of a Member State, or in the territory of a third state whose bureau of insurers has joined the green card system.

(4) The Office shall provide substitute performance to the injured party on the basis of their written request.

(5) The injured party shall not have the right to substitute performance against the Office if

- a) they have already submitted a claim for damages directly to the insurer pursuant to § 9b (1) who concluded the liability insurance contract concerning the vehicle whose operation caused the damage, or directly to the insurer's claims representative pursuant to § 9b, and if they received a statement from either of them within 3 months from the date of submission of this claim,

- b) they have already submitted a claim for damages directly to the insurance company pursuant to § 9c who concluded the liability insurance contract concerning the vehicle whose operation caused the damage, or directly to the claims representative of this insurance company pursuant to § 9c, and if they received a statement from either of them within 3 months from the date of submission of this claim, or

- c) court proceedings have been initiated in the given matter directly against the insurer pursuant to § 9b (1) or against the insurance company pursuant to § 9c.

(6) The Office is obliged to perform to the injured party or to state the reasons for which it reduced or refused performance, within 2 months from the date of receipt of the request for substitute performance. This request shall be shelved if, by the time of its settlement, the insurer pursuant to § 9b (1), or the insurance company pursuant to § 9c, directly or through an appointed claims representative pursuant to § 9b or § 9c, provides the injured party with compensation for the incurred damage or informs them of the reasons for which it reduces its performance or for which it refuses to perform.

(7) The Office shall, without undue delay after receiving a request for substitute performance, inform in writing

- a) the insurer pursuant to § 9b (1) or the insurance company pursuant to § 9c or their claims representative pursuant to § 9b or § 9c,

- b) the body entrusted with handling requests for substitute performance in the Member State of the registered office of the insurance company pursuant to § 9c, which concluded the liability insurance contract concerning the vehicle whose operation caused the damage, or

- c) the person who is obliged to compensate for the damage, if known,

that it has received a request from the injured party and that it will settle this request within 2 months from the date of its receipt.

(8) The Office has the right to reimbursement of the paid amount of substitute performance from the body entrusted with handling requests for substitute performance in the Member State of the registered office of the insurance company pursuant to § 9c, which concluded the liability insurance contract concerning the vehicle whose operation caused the damage.

(9) By paying the substitute performance, the rights of the injured party against the person who caused the damage, or against their insurer pursuant to § 9a or § 9b (1), or their insurance company pursuant to § 9c, shall pass to the Office.

(10) If the competent authority of a Member State other than the Czech Republic has provided substitute performance to an injured party with a domicile or registered office in the same Member State in accordance with European Community law^27) and has asserted its right to reimbursement of this performance against the Office, the Office shall satisfy such asserted right without undue delay, provided that the vehicle whose operation caused the damage had its usual place of garaging in the territory of the Czech Republic at the time of the loss event.

p24b.text:
(1) The Office shall also provide substitute performance from the Guarantee Fund to an injured party with a domicile or registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic if

- a) it is not possible to identify the vehicle whose operation caused damage in the territory of a Member State other than the Czech Republic, or

- b) within 2 months from the date of the damage caused by the operation of a vehicle in the territory of a Member State other than the Czech Republic, it is not possible to identify the insurance company that incurred the obligation to provide insurance benefits for this damage.

(2) The Office has the right to reimbursement of the paid amount of substitute performance pursuant to paragraph 1

- a) against the guarantee fund in the Member State where the damage occurred, if the vehicle whose operation caused the damage cannot be identified,

- b) against the guarantee fund of the Member State where the damage occurred, if the damage was caused by the operation of a vehicle that has its usual place of garaging in the territory of a non-Member State,

- c) against the guarantee fund in the Member State in whose territory the vehicle whose operation caused the damage has its usual place of garaging, if the insurance company that incurred the obligation to provide insurance benefits for the damage caused by such a vehicle cannot be identified.

(3) The provisions of § 24a (9) and (10) shall apply mutatis mutandis in the case of substitute performance pursuant to paragraph 1.

p24c.heading: Contribution to the Damage Prevention Fund
p24c.text: The Office shall decide on the transfer of funds from the Guarantee Fund to the Damage Prevention Fund up to the difference between the income and expenditure of the Guarantee Fund for the past calendar year, taking into account the increase or decrease in the Guarantee Fund's outstanding liabilities.

p25.heading: Supervision of the Office's activities
p25.text:
(1) The administration of the Guarantee Fund pursuant to § 18 (2) (a) and other activities of the Bureau pursuant to § 18 (2) (b), (d) and (f) and (6), (7), (9) and (11) are subject to supervision exercised by the Czech National Bank in accordance with the Act regulating insurance.

(2) In exercising supervision, the Czech National Bank may impose remedial measures on the Bureau to eliminate identified deficiencies. If the Bureau fails to comply with the imposed remedial measures within the period set by the Czech National Bank, or if these deficiencies recur in its activities, the Czech National Bank may impose a fine on the Bureau, up to a maximum of CZK 10 million.

Part Four

Joint and Transitional Provisions

p26.heading: Joint Provisions
p26.text:
(1) If a vehicle is co-owned by multiple persons, the obligations under this Act shall be fulfilled by the co-owner who has been authorized in writing by the vehicle's co-owners to do so. If no co-owner has been authorized in writing, all co-owners shall fulfill the obligations under the first sentence jointly and severally. In the event of the dissolution of a legal entity, the obligations of the vehicle owner under this Act shall be performed by its legal successor; if the legal entity dissolves without a legal successor, by the liquidator. If insolvency proceedings are underway, addressing the insolvency or impending insolvency of the vehicle owner, the obligations of the vehicle owner under this Act shall be performed by the insolvency administrator after their appointment, if they have acquired the authority to dispose of the vehicle owner's estate.

(2) In the event of the death of the vehicle owner, the obligations of the vehicle owner under this Act shall be performed, until the resolution on the acquisition of inheritance becomes final, by their heir, or in the case of multiple heirs, by the one authorized by the other heirs, or by the legatee. If no one has been authorized, these obligations shall be performed by the estate administrator. If no one among multiple heirs has been authorized and no estate administrator has been appointed, the obligations of the vehicle owner shall be performed by the heirs jointly and severally. In the event of the death of the policyholder, the rights and obligations arising from liability insurance shall pass to the heirs jointly and severally until the resolution on the acquisition of inheritance becomes final.

(3) If a vehicle is separated from ownership into a trust fund, the obligations under this Act shall be fulfilled by the trustee.

p26a.heading: Transitional Provisions
p26a.text: The powers assigned to the municipal office of a municipality with extended powers or to city districts under this Act constitute the exercise of delegated powers.

p27.text:
(1) An insurance contract may be concluded with effect no earlier than January 1, 2000.

(2) The obligation under § 1 (2) arises on January 1, 2000.

(3) For loss events that occurred before January 1, 2000, and claims arising therefrom, the existing legal regulations shall apply.

(4) An insurer who, before January 1, 2000, concluded an insurance contract with a policyholder concerning a vehicle that was registered in the vehicle register before January 1, 2000, is obliged to submit to the Bureau, within 5 working days after the end of the calendar month, an overview of the insurance documents issued for the entire preceding calendar month and the data contained therein.

p28.text:
(1) The Ministry shall convene the first assembly of all members of the Bureau within 4 months of the effective date of this Act.

(2) The costs of the Bureau's activities in 1999 shall be covered by its members in the form of an extraordinary contribution, the amount of which shall be determined by the assembly of members at its first meeting. This contribution is payable within one month from the date it was determined by the assembly of members.

(3) The amount of the Guarantee Fund's resources must be at least

- a) as of January 1, 2000, 3% of the total volume of insurance benefits from statutory motor third party liability insurance (hereinafter referred to as “statutory insurance”) paid out in 1998,

- b) as of January 1, 2001, 5% of the total volume of insurance benefits paid out from statutory insurance in 1999,

- c) as of July 1, 2001, 20% of the total volume of benefits paid out from the Guarantee Fund in the previous accounting period.

p29.text:
(1) The rights and obligations of Česká pojišťovna a.s. arising from statutory insurance shall transfer to the Bureau on January 1, 2000. These rights and obligations shall be asserted and exercised by Česká pojišťovna a.s. on behalf and for the account of the Bureau, including representing the Bureau before courts in decisions concerning rights or obligations arising from statutory insurance, especially in connection with providing compensation to injured parties and asserting the right to recover paid amounts from the insured. For the purposes of an agreement with an injured party pursuant to § 449a of the Civil Code, in these cases, the Bureau shall be considered the liable person even if the injured party does not have a direct claim for performance against the Bureau. Česká pojišťovna a.s. shall maintain separate accounting records for this activity from the accounting records for other activities.

(2) Česká pojišťovna a.s. shall transfer to a special account of the Bureau, by the last day of the month following the auditor's verification of the financial statements for 1999, an amount corresponding to the amount of technical reserves created from premiums for statutory insurance received up to December 31, 1999, after deducting the amount of insurance benefits paid out from statutory insurance from January 1, 2000, to the date of transfer. The funds deposited in this account may only be used to cover liabilities from statutory insurance and related settlement costs. If the funds in this account are insufficient for this purpose, the coverage of liabilities from statutory insurance and related settlement costs shall be borne by the Bureau.

(3) Česká pojišťovna a.s. is obliged to submit, within one month after the first meeting of the assembly of members of the Bureau, a database of persons who, as of that date, paid premiums for statutory insurance in 1999, and statistical data on the development of statutory insurance for the last 5 years.

(4) Česká pojišťovna a.s. shall submit to the Bureau, by March 31 of the current year, a report on its activities pursuant to the second sentence of paragraph 1 and its accounting.

(5) The activities of the Bureau and Česká pojišťovna a.s. pursuant to paragraphs 1 to 4 are subject to insurance supervision under a special legal regulation,^10) whereby the provisions of this special legal regulation shall apply mutatis mutandis to the exercise of supervision over these activities of the Bureau and Česká pojišťovna a.s.

p30.text: In Act No. 185/1991 Sb., on insurance, as amended by Act No. 320/1993 Sb., Act No. 60/1995 Sb., and Act No. 149/1995 Sb., § 26 is hereby repealed.

p31.text: In Act No. 12/1997 Sb., on the safety and fluidity of road traffic, § 14, including footnotes No. 14) and 15), is hereby repealed.

p34.text:
This Act shall enter into force on the date of its promulgation, with the exception of the provisions of § 1 para. 2, § 15 para. 1, 7, 8 and 9, § 16, 17, Part Two, Three, Four and Five, which shall enter into force on 1 January 2000.
